name: Laguna de la Mata and Río Segura Loop
name_original: Laguna de La Mata – Circular por el Río Segura y las Cuevas del Rodeo
region: Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja
description: This moderate 23.1-mile touring cycling route explores the Laguna de La Mata and Río Segura, featuring salt lakes and the Cuevas del Rodeo.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja traverse a unique natural park characterized by two prominent saltwater lagoons: the greenish Laguna de la Mata and the pink Laguna de Torrevieja, separated by a land strip. The terrain is generally flat and semi-arid, featuring salt pans, tamarisk, and pine and eucalyptus trees. Routes are well-signposted and easy to navigate, making them suitable for various fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja The most popular touring cycling route is Laguna de la Mata and Río Segura Loop, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Laguna de la Mata and the surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laguna Salada de Torrevieja loop from Hospital San Jaime, a moderate 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route circles the striking pink Laguna de Torrevieja, providing unique scenery and birdwatching opportunities. Local touring cyclists also love the View of La Pedrera Reservoir – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from San Luis, a 35.3 miles (56.8 km) trail leading through the area around La Pedrera Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Touring cycling in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja is defined by saltwater lagoons, salt pans, and semi-arid terrain, with routes often shaded by pine and eucalyptus forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family outings to moderate distances. The routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 2,900 reviews. More than 12,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: The terrain in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja is generally flat and semi-arid, making it ideal for touring cycling. You'll find well-signposted routes traversing salt pans, tamarisk, and areas shaded by pine and eucalyptus trees. The routes are typically easy to navigate, suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja offers a good selection of routes for beginners and those seeking an easy ride. Out of over 420 touring cycling routes, 131 are classified as easy, providing gentle paths through the unique natural park landscape.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the region?
A: There are over 420 touring cycling routes available in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 131 easy, 220 moderate, and 71 difficult options.

Q: What unique natural features can I expect to see while cycling in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: While cycling, you'll encounter the region's most distinctive features: the greenish &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; and the striking pink &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3986767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pink Lake of Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;. These saltwater lagoons are separated by a land strip and are part of the larger &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/771712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. The pink hue of Laguna de Torrevieja is particularly vibrant during warmer months due to micro-algae.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The natural park is a significant wetland system and a special bird protection zone (ZEPA). Cyclists often spot flamingos, black-necked grebes, black-winged stilts, and other waterfowl. There are dedicated bird observatories and viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5589759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Hut – Parque Natural de las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;, strategically placed throughout the park to enhance this experience.

Q: Can I bring my family for a touring cycling trip in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: Yes, the flat terrain and well-maintained, often paved, surfaces make many routes suitable for family outings. The park's dedicated cycling routes offer a peaceful journey through diverse landscapes, perfect for cyclists of all ages.

Q: Are touring cycling routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja dog-friendly?
A: While the natural park is a protected area, many routes are accessible for dogs on a leash. It's always best to check specific park regulations or route descriptions on komoot for any restrictions, especially concerning wildlife protection zones.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: The warmer months are particularly good for witnessing the vibrant pink color of Laguna de Torrevieja. However, the semi-arid climate means cycling is generally pleasant outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn offer comfortable temperatures for exploring the routes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952445615/laguna-de-la-mata-and-rio-segura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Mata and Río Segura Loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31417991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Salada de Torrevieja loop from Hospital San Jaime&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the pink lagoon.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 2,900 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique scenery, the ease of navigation, and the opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: What is the typical length of touring cycling routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja?
A: Route lengths vary, but many popular touring cycling routes range from moderate distances of around 35-40 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952445615/laguna-de-la-mata-and-rio-segura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Mata and Río Segura Loop&lt;/a&gt; (37.2 km), to longer options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34508702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Pedrera Reservoir – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from San Luis&lt;/a&gt; (56.8 km).

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at various access points to the natural park and near popular trailheads. The Visitor's Interpretation Center near Laguna de La Mata often serves as a good starting point with parking facilities.

Q: How can I reach the touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: Public transport options to the Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja area are available, primarily through bus services connecting from nearby towns like Torrevieja. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes that stop close to the natural park entrances or specific cycling trailheads.
tips: Great tour through a beautiful, quiet landscape. Wonderful to look at, beautiful views, successful round that is really worth it.

The paths are clearly visible from the car park, you need a bit of riding technique, but it works.😉 Beginners should use the road at the roundabout to get to the coast or just enjoy the beautiful views. A small, well-intentioned tip!
For those who like the sea it is beautiful, but then you have to go back. ;)

The El Rodeo caves were dug out of the slope of a raised area between the 18th and 20th centuries to create housing. They were built by experienced miners who moved to Rojales specifically for this project. Their knowledge undoubtedly made it possible to create this special urban landscape! 

The El Rodeo caves are now home to craft businesses and there is even a collection on the history of these caves.

Beautiful stretch of road in the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de la Mata. Wonderful views, a tour of both lagoons is recommended and doable. Can be done without any great difficulty. Alternative routes are possible, but not a must.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Laguna de La Mata – Circular by the Río Segura and the Cuevas del Rodeo trail?
faqs.answer: The route is largely flat, but the terrain is quite varied. You'll find mostly paved surfaces, but also some unpaved segments that can be challenging, with stony sections or deeper, "spongy" sand. Wider tires are advantageous for these parts, and you might need to dismount and push your bike occasionally. If there's been recent heavy rain, some areas could be muddy.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: You can find parking near the Natural Park of Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja. For accessing the Cuevas del Rodeo section, there is parking available in Rojales, which is a good starting point if you want to explore the cave houses first.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Natural Park of Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route within the Natural Park of Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja. However, always respect park regulations and signage.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Laguna de La Mata – Circular by the Río Segura and the Cuevas del Rodeo route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a mix of natural beauty and cultural highlights. You'll cycle through the La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Natural Park, seeing the stunning salt lakes, including the reddish Laguna de La Mata. Key cultural stops include the fascinating Rodeo Caves in Rojales, which are 18th-century cave houses now used as art studios. You'll also pass the Pink Lighthouse at the Mouth of the Segura River, Guardamar del Segura and enjoy views from Mirador El Moncayo and The Three Crosses Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May), to avoid the intense summer heat. This period is also excellent for birdwatching, especially for flamingos. If you're interested in the bohemian craft market at the Cuevas del Rodeo, plan your visit for the first Sunday of the month.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the route is largely flat, its "moderate" difficulty rating and varied terrain with unpaved, stony, and sandy sections might be challenging for younger children or those less experienced on bikes. Some parts may require dismounting and pushing. It's best suited for families with older, more confident cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this route passes through a Natural Park, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Be prepared for varied terrain that might not be comfortable for all dogs, especially the stony or sandy sections.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for birdwatching along the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route goes through the Natural Park of Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja, which is a significant bird habitat. There are numerous viewpoints and bird observatories along the way, offering excellent opportunities to spot flamingos and other aquatic birds, especially during migration seasons.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Guardamar del Segura - Torrevieja trail, El Camí IT5TR3 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Camí de la Gola, and the Corredor Verde del Río Segura, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to rest or have a picnic along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is well-maintained and features designated picnic areas where you can take a break, enjoy a snack, and soak in the tranquil surroundings. These spots are perfect for resting, especially after navigating some of the more challenging unpaved sections.

faqs.question: What makes the Cuevas del Rodeo a unique highlight on this route?
faqs.answer: The Cuevas del Rodeo are truly unique. These 18th-century cave houses, originally carved by miners, have been transformed into vibrant art studios and a cultural attraction. You can admire their distinctive white-washed exteriors and bright blue doors and windows. From this elevated area, you also get panoramic views of Rojales and the surrounding agricultural lands. On the first Sunday of each month, they host a bohemian craft market.
